text.title: pytest Mocking — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-pytest-mocking is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.reason: Root cause: Patch has no effect; real send_mail still runs. Fix: Patch the use site: 'apps.orders.services.send_mail'.

antipattern.description: mocker.patch('x', return_value=...) for an async callable.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: RuntimeWarning: coroutine was never awaited. Fix: Use AsyncMock or mocker.patch(..., new=AsyncMock(return_value=...)).
Root cause: Mock accepts any signature; silent drift. Fix: autospec=True (or create_autospec).
Root cause: Tests the mock, not the queryset. Fix: Use real DB fixture + factory boy.
Root cause: Brittle assertion; breaks on minor refactors. Fix: Use call_args.args / call_args.kwargs.
